mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p>0.10) against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals imminent labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 55% in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or flat 60-day return in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient real economic activity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in > 60% of tightening-cycle observations, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion that supports near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias (accuracy < 52%) across 10-year out-of-sample tests, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.5 percent year-over-year forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 55% of high-inflation triggered periods, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions (KS test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a liquidity crunch signal immediate consumer distress and credit contraction, triggering rapid risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of liquidity-crunch observations, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when high-yield spreads are below 300 bps.
